title: Chile Submits $2.8Bn Hydrogen Tax Credit Bill to Congress
content: Chileâ€™s government has formally submitted a US$2.8 billion tax credit bill to the Chamber of Deputies, aiming to catalyse demand for domestically produced green hydrogen and its derivatives. The legislationâ€”tabled on August 19 by the Ministry of Financeâ€”would distribute credits via six competitive auctions between 2025 and 2030, targeting companies that purchase green hydrogen, ammonia, or e-methanol from newly built national projects. The programme is designed to front-load the incentives in early years, with gradually decreasing caps on eligible claims to accelerate market growth.
author: Analysts Account
